title: Respiration & the Respiratory System
subject: science
introduction: Respiration is a chemical process happening in every cell of your body right now. It's how your cells release energy from food. Don't confuse it with breathing—breathing is just the physical movement of air in and out of your lungs!

sections.title: What is Respiration?
content: Respiration is a chemical reaction that happens inside cells
It breaks down glucose (sugar) to release energy
This energy is used for all body activities: moving, thinking, growing
Happens in every cell, not just in lungs
Two types: Aerobic (with oxygen) and Anaerobic (without oxygen)
Aerobic respiration is much more efficient (releases more energy)

sections.title: Aerobic Respiration Equation
content: Glucose + Oxygen → Carbon Dioxide + Water + Energy
This equation shows the chemical reaction
Glucose comes from food we eat
Oxygen comes from air we breathe
CO₂ and water are waste products we get rid of

sections.title: Structure of Respiratory System
content: Bronchi: Trachea branches into two bronchi (one per lung)
Bronchioles: Bronchi branch into smaller tubes
Alveoli: Tiny air sacs where gas exchange happens (millions per lung!)

diagram:
RESPIRATORY SYSTEM PATH:

Nose/Mouth
    ↓ (warm air)
Trachea (windpipe)
    ↓
Bronchi (splits into 2)
    ↓           ↓
Left Lung    Right Lung
    ↓           ↓
Bronchioles  Bronchioles
    ↓           ↓
Alveoli (gas exchange!)
    ↓
Oxygen → Blood
CO₂ ← Blood

flashcards.a: A chemical process that breaks down glucose to release energy in cells
In the alveoli (tiny air sacs in lungs)
Glucose + Oxygen → Carbon Dioxide + Water + Energy

flashcards.q: What's the difference between respiration and breathing?
flashcards.a: Breathing is physical movement of air; respiration is chemical energy release
To break down glucose and release energy in cells
The windpipe that carries air from mouth/nose to lungs
Respiration with oxygen that releases the most energy
Respiration without oxygen that releases less energy
The part of cells where respiration happens and energy is released
Adenosine triphosphate - the energy molecule cells use for work

quiz.q: The equation for aerobic respiration is...
What is the difference between respiration and breathing?
opts: Respiration is chemical, breathing is physical

quiz.q: How many times does respiration happen?
Which gas is released from cells during respiration?
What is the purpose of aerobic respiration?

Key Terms.Respiration: Breaking down glucose for energy (happens in cells)
Key Terms.Alveoli: Tiny air sacs where gas exchange occurs

cheatsheet.Remember: Every cell respires, not just lungs!
cheatsheet.Exam Focus: Know the difference between respiration and breathing. Know where gas exchange happens.
